in 2004, Jeanne and Francis struck the east coast of Florida back to back - debris that was waiting for cleanup became airborne, and things that were already loose like roofs, fences, trees, and electrical infrastructure were easily damaged. The 2nd hit will often cause worse damage regardless of storm strength because of these principles.
